title: Beneath the skies / Tian kong xia : Zhong da ren fan song Zhong yun dong fang tan ji
originalTitle: 天空下 : 中大人反送中運動訪談集
description: Beneath the skies / Tian kong xia : Zhong da ren fan song Zhong yun dong fang tan ji is a book by Shan cheng ji shi. The source page records removal or withholding in Correctional Services Department collections amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure.
descriptionParagraphs: Beneath the skies / Tian kong xia : Zhong da ren fan song Zhong yun dong fang tan ji is a book by Shan cheng ji shi. The source page records removal or withholding in Correctional Services Department collections amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure.

Even with sparse bibliographic metadata, the work is interesting because its very availability became part of a struggle over Hong Kong's public sphere. As a book, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Hong Kong. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: The source page records removal or withholding in Correctional Services Department collections amid the post-2020 tightening of Hong Kong's public reading infrastructure. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

banningParagraphs: Beneath the skies / Tian kong xia : Zhong da ren fan song Zhong yun dong fang tan ji entered censorship debates as a book associated with hong kong, political memory, and public argument. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around national security and political dissent.

The earliest event currently captured here is 2021-12-1 in Hong Kong, where Hong Kong public libraries and other government-managed collections removed from government-managed collections. counterReadings.title: The Origins of Totalitarianism
counterReadings.author: Hannah Arendt
counterReadings.note: A foundational analysis of state terror, propaganda, and ideological conformity.

counterReadings.title: On Tyranny
counterReadings.author: Timothy Snyder
counterReadings.note: A short modern guide to resisting authoritarian politics and controlled public discourse.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for comparing older obscenity, heresy, and political bans with modern free-speech disputes.
